target: §7.11 Aetheric Refraction: three structural argument improvements — k=0.097→0.18 fix (ISS-2398), dispersion wavelength-dependence caveat (ISS-2400), WIN-017/WIN-026 shared-property clause (ISS-2402)

patches.find_pattern: the equatorial ring table value n = 1.40 requires a coefficient of ~0.097 — inconsistent with both the current 0.20 and the older ~0.27.
patches.replace_pattern: the equatorial ring table value n = 1.40 at that radius requires a coefficient of ~0.18 — inconsistent with the current 0.20 and the older ~0.27.
patches.description: ISS-2398: correct the k value. At r ≈ 10,008 km (equatorial ring), using the published H at that radius, achieving n=1.40 requires k≈0.18, not k≈0.097. The 0.097 figure is internally inconsistent with the dome's own formula at this radius. The inconsistency argument survives — 0.18 is still not 0.20 or 0.27 — so the piecemeal-table conclusion is unchanged.

patches.find_pattern: Stars near the southern horizon resolve as sharp points — no rainbow smearing despite a predicted n of 2.84 (for context, diamond at n = 2.42 produces the visible "fire" of cut gemstones).
patches.replace_pattern: Stars near the southern horizon resolve as sharp points — no rainbow smearing despite a predicted n of 2.84 (for context, diamond at n = 2.42 produces the visible "fire" of cut gemstones; note: this signature requires wavelength-dependent n — the dome has not committed to this, making dispersion a conditional rather than necessary prediction; the angular-position shift and total-internal-reflection signatures below hold regardless of wavelength-dependence and are the stronger falsifiers).
patches.description: ISS-2400: add wavelength-dependence caveat to the dispersion falsification. The dome's n(r) formula contains no wavelength variable; a constant-n medium produces zero chromatic dispersion. Dispersion is therefore only a conditional prediction. The caveat is added as a parenthetical; the remaining test signatures (angular shift, TIR cutoff, ranging disagreement) are unaffected and remain the primary falsifiers.

patches.find_pattern: Four further WINs require an equivalent unfalsifiable correction to reconcile dome geometry with observation, even though the dome's text frames them differently:
patches.replace_pattern: Four further WINs require an equivalent unfalsifiable correction — each invoking a free dome-specific parameter with no globe-model counterpart — to reconcile dome geometry with observation, even though the dome's text frames them differently:
patches.description: ISS-2402: add the shared structural property that makes WIN-017/WIN-026 equivalent to n(r). Each of the four WINs invokes a parameter (firmament wobble, perspective compression, H(r)-derived axial tilt, exponential curvature) that is absent from the globe model and can be tuned freely — exactly the structural property that makes n(r) unfalsifiable. Adding this clause makes the equivalence claim self-supporting rather than asserted.

tldr_evidence: Three targeted prose fixes to §7.11: (1) k=0.097→0.18 correction is verified by the dome's own n(r) formula at the equatorial radius — the inconsistency argument survives with the corrected number; (2) dispersion caveat is logically necessary since chromatic dispersion requires wavelength-dependent n, which the dome has not specified; (3) WIN-017/WIN-026 equivalence now names the shared structural property (free parameter with no globe-model counterpart) that makes the equivalence claim coherent.
tldr_verdict: No verdict change. All three fixes strengthen or clarify existing arguments. ISS-2398 corrects a specific numerical error without weakening the piecemeal-table conclusion. ISS-2400 adds a logical qualification that actually makes the dispersion falsification more rigorous. ISS-2402 adds explanatory content that makes the WIN-equivalence claim self-supporting.
